messages.content:
You are the ComfyUI agent in a sidebar panel, driving the user's live ComfyUI graph and server. Answer in normal Markdown.

You have exactly six tools:
- list_tools / describe_tool / call_tool — the headless ComfyUI server (~200 capabilities: generate images/video/audio, models, custom nodes, queue, diagnostics). Flow: list_tools {"search": ...} → describe_tool {"name": ...} → call_tool {"name": ..., "args": {...}}.
- panel_list_tools / panel_describe_tool / panel_call_tool — the user's LIVE canvas (read the graph, add/wire nodes, set widgets, run, screenshots, show media). Same flow.

Rules:
- Catalog entries are tool NAMES, not data. Finish every task by actually running tools; never invent results.
- Describe a tool before its first call so you use the right parameters. If a call errors, read the error — it includes the expected schema — fix the args and retry.
- To see or show any generated image/video, run the panel_show_media tool via panel_call_tool.
- Workflows with API nodes cost the user PAID credits; local-GPU workflows are free. Ask before anything that might spend credits.
Start a new workflow for me.

Live-canvas panel tools — 21 of 65. Next: panel_describe_tool {"name": ...} then panel_call_tool.
- panel_get_graph: Read the workflow the user is CURRENTLY VIEWING on their canvas (root graph or an opened subgraph — 'viewing' says which): node ids, types, titles, widget valu…
- panel_graph_outline: Read a COMPACT, dependency-ordered TEXT MAP of the workflow the user is viewing — the FASTEST way to UNDERSTAND a graph (especially a big loaded pack/template)…
- panel_find_nodes: SEARCH the workflow the user is CURRENTLY VIEWING for nodes matching filters — the right way to PINPOINT a node (a specific loader, sampler, save, switch) in a…
- panel_clear: Remove EVERY node from the user's open graph — only for an explicit 'clear/reset the canvas'.
- panel_strip_workflow: Strip a workflow to a clean, flat, RESOLVED graph — Get/Set buses, Reroutes, subgraph definitions, and bypassed/muted nodes all collapsed into real connections…
- panel_slice_workflow: Slice ONE pipeline out of a toggle-template workflow (built with rgthree 'Fast Groups Bypasser/Muter' — one graph holding many pipelines, only one active at a…
- panel_load_workflow: Load a full ComfyUI workflow onto the live canvas in one shot (replaces the current graph).
- panel_run: Queue the workflow the user has OPEN — exactly like them pressing Queue Prompt (current widget values, the live graph they can see).
- panel_get_content_mode: Query the persistent adult-content (NSFW) consent state for this user.
- panel_save_workflow: Save the user's open workflow PROGRAMMATICALLY — no Save/Rename dialog ever pops.
- panel_list_workflows: List the user's OPEN workflow tabs and which one is active (path, filename, modified, persisted).
- panel_new_workflow: Open a brand-new BLANK workflow in a NEW TAB.
- panel_open_workflow: Open / switch to a workflow by path or filename (from panel_list_workflows).
- panel_rename_workflow: Rename a workflow (the active one, or the one matching `path`).
- panel_close_workflow: Close a workflow tab (the active one, or the one matching `path`).
- panel_copy_nodes: Copy nodes from the user's open graph to the clipboard.
- panel_paste_nodes: Paste the clipboard (from a prior panel_copy_nodes) onto the user's CURRENTLY OPEN graph — including a graph in a DIFFERENT workflow, which is how you merge/co…
- panel_save_subgraph: Save a SUBGRAPH node to the user's reusable blueprint LIBRARY (publish), so it can be dropped into any workflow later.
- panel_add_subgraph: Add a saved subgraph blueprint (from panel_list_subgraphs) onto the user's open graph by name (or full 'SubgraphBlueprint.<name>' type).
- panel_set_node_mode: Set a node's EXECUTION MODE on the user's open graph — active, bypass, or mute — and return { node_id, mode, previous_mode }.
- panel_screenshot: Render the workflow the user is currently viewing (root graph, or the open subgraph) to a PNG and return it as an IMAGE so you can SEE the layout.

comfyui-mcp tool catalog — 55 of 113 tools (filtered). Workflow: pick a tool → describe_tool {"name": ...} for its parameters → call_tool {"name": ..., "args": {...}}.
## workflows (11)
- enqueue_workflow: Submit a ComfyUI workflow for execution and return immediately with the prompt_id and queue position.
- rerun_generation: Re-run the workflow behind a previous generation.
- get_system_stats: Get system information from the connected ComfyUI server: GPU device(s), total/free VRAM, ComfyUI/Python/PyTorch versions, and OS details.
- get_queue: Get the current ComfyUI execution queue: the job running now plus all pending jobs, each with its prompt_id and position.
- get_queued_workflow: Return the full workflow payload for one PENDING queue item by prompt_id.
- move_queued_job: Move a PENDING ComfyUI queue item to the front or back by removing it and re-enqueuing its saved workflow payload.
- edit_queued_job: Edit a PENDING ComfyUI queue item by removing it and re-enqueuing an updated workflow.
- get_job_status: Check the status of ONE ComfyUI job by its prompt_id (the id returned by enqueue_workflow).
- run_workflow_url: Read (and optionally execute) a shared ComfyUI workflow from a URL.
- extract_workflow_dependencies: Analyze a ComfyUI workflow (API JSON) and determine which custom node packs it requires.
- install_workflow_dependencies: Resolve and install the custom node packs a ComfyUI workflow requires via ComfyUI-Manager.

## workflow-authoring (17)
- visualize_workflow: Convert a ComfyUI workflow JSON into a Mermaid flowchart diagram.
- mermaid_to_workflow: Convert a Mermaid flowchart diagram back into a ComfyUI workflow JSON.
- visualize_workflow_hierarchical: Visualize a large ComfyUI workflow as a hierarchical diagram.
- create_workflow: Create a ready-to-run ComfyUI API-format workflow from a built-in template (txt2img, img2img, upscale, inpaint, controlnet, ip_adapter, ace_step_15, stable_aud…
- modify_workflow: Apply modification operations to an existing ComfyUI workflow.
- get_node_info: Query a running ComfyUI server's /object_info endpoint for installed node type definitions (inputs, outputs, category, description).
- validate_workflow: Validate a ComfyUI workflow without executing it.
- list_workflows: List the filenames of workflows saved in the connected ComfyUI server's user library (the same workflows visible in the ComfyUI web UI).
- get_workflow: Load a saved workflow and return its raw JSON.
- strip_workflow: Strip a workflow to a clean, flat API graph — resolving Get/Set buses, Reroutes, subgraph definitions, and bypassed/muted nodes into real connections (the 'de-…
- slice_workflow: Slice ONE pipeline out of a toggle-template workflow — the kind built with rgthree 'Fast Groups Bypasser/Muter' where one graph holds many pipelines and only o…
- save_workflow: Save a workflow JSON to the connected ComfyUI server's user library so it appears in the ComfyUI web UI.
- analyze_workflow: Load a saved workflow and return a structured analysis — sections, node settings, connections, and data flow.
- workflow_to_dsl: Convert a ComfyUI API-format workflow into a compact, human/LLM-readable DSL — easier to read and edit than raw JSON, and round-trips losslessly back via dsl_t…
- dsl_to_workflow: Convert the compact workflow DSL (see workflow_to_dsl) back into executable ComfyUI API-format JSON.
- lock_workflow: Capture a provenance lock for a saved workflow so it can be exactly reproduced later.
- verify_workflow_lock: Compare a saved workflow's lock file against the current state of the local install and report drift.

## custom-nodes (1)
- scaffold_custom_node: Generate a new ComfyUI custom-node pack from a template into <COMFYUI_PATH>/custom_nodes/<name>/.

## skills-config (8)
- generate_node_skill: Generate a Claude skill (SKILL.md) documenting a ComfyUI custom node pack: its nodes, inputs/outputs, and example workflows.
- get_defaults: Return the merged view of generation defaults with per-source attribution.
- list_skills: List the bundled ComfyUI model-family + workflow skills shipped with comfyui-mcp (name + description for each).
- read_skill: Return the full body of a bundled skill's SKILL.md by name (discover names with list_skills).
- list_packs: List the bundled installer packs under packs/ — one-command setups for a model family: custom nodes + model weights (manifest.yaml) PLUS a ready workflow.json…
- list_workflow_templates: List the OFFICIAL ComfyUI workflow templates available on the connected ComfyUI (the comfyui-workflow-templates package + any custom-node-provided templates),…
- read_pack_workflow: Return a bundled pack's ready workflow.json graph by pack name (discover names + which packs have a workflow with list_packs).
- check_workflow_runtime: Determine whether a workflow runs on the user's OWN GPU (LOCAL — free) or uses hosted API NODES (PAID api credits).

## diagnostics (2)
- get_history: Get execution history for a ComfyUI prompt.
- report_issue: Build a ready-to-open GitHub issue link for a bug/problem you hit (ComfyUI, a workflow, a model, custom nodes, or comfyui-mcp itself).

## server (1)
- configure_manager: Configure ComfyUI-Manager settings, mirroring `comfy-cli manager` subcommands.

## images-assets (6)
- upload_audio: Upload a local audio file (.wav, .mp3, .flac, .ogg, .m4a, .aac) to the connected ComfyUI's input/ directory via the HTTP /upload/image endpoint for use in audi…
- workflow_from_image: Extract embedded ComfyUI workflow metadata from a PNG file.
- view_image: Fetch a registered asset's bytes and return them as an inline image so the agent can see the result.
- list_assets: List recently generated assets from the in-memory registry, newest-first.
- get_asset_metadata: Get full provenance for a registered asset including the workflow snapshot that produced it.
- regenerate: Re-enqueue the workflow that produced an existing asset, optionally applying parameter overrides.

## generation (9)
- suggest_settings: Recommend concrete, proven sampler/scheduler/steps/CFG (and denoise/shift/LoRA) settings derived from THIS MCP server's local generation-history database (popu…
- generation_stats: Show statistics from this MCP server's local generation-history database (populated as you run workflows; not from ComfyUI itself): total generations, count of…
- generate_image: Generate an image from a text prompt — the high-level entry point.
- generate_audio: Generate audio from a text prompt — supports ACE Step 1.5 and Stable Audio 3 model families.
- generate_video: Generate a short video from a text prompt (text-to-video), or animate a start image (image-to-video when `image` is given) — the high-level entry point.
- remove_background: Remove an image's background, returning a transparent (RGBA) cutout — the high-level entry point.
- upscale_image: Upscale an image with an ESRGAN super-resolution model — the high-level entry point.
- generate_with_ip_adapter: Generate an image guided by a reference image's style/subject via IP-Adapter, plus a text prompt.
- generate_with_api_node: Build a minimal single-node workflow that runs a chosen API/partner node with the provided inputs and enqueue it.

This is a FILTERED view (58 tools hidden). If nothing here fits the task, call list_tools again with a broader search or no filter.

Your new blank workflow is ready! 🎨

A fresh **"Untitled"** tab has been opened on your canvas. You can now:

- **Ask me to build a workflow** — just describe what you want to generate (e.g., "a txt2img workflow with SDXL," "an img2img with ControlNet," "a video generation pipeline")
- **Use templates** — I can load a ready-made template (txt2img, img2img, upscale, inpaint, ControlNet, IP-Adapter, audio, video, and more)
- **Add nodes manually** — tell me which nodes to place and wire up

What would you like to create?
